title: ProtocolLab Public Report Contract
purpose: Define the public report payload and supporting metadata contracts used by ProtocolLab report consumers.
scope: This specification covers public report identity, claim boundaries, evidence preservation, artifact references, and public-safety rules. It does not define a runner, upload workflow, object-store layout, site importer, or hosted lab implementation.
context: ProtocolLab consumers need public-safe report documents that preserve evidence semantics without relying on private lab state.

requirements.title: Schema-versioned public report
requirements.statement: A public ProtocolLab report MUST declare a stable schema version and validate against the matching public report schema.

requirements.title: Evidence preservation
requirements.statement: A public ProtocolLab report MUST preserve validation failures, unsupported outcomes, unavailable outcomes, diagnostic labels, and non-publishable claim status.

requirements.title: No claim upgrades
requirements.statement: A public ProtocolLab report MUST NOT infer publishable, benchmark, or verified claims from throughput, duration, implementation identity, executor identity, or private lab knowledge.

requirements.title: Public-safe artifacts
requirements.statement: A public ProtocolLab report MUST reference only artifacts that are safe for public consumption and MUST NOT expose private paths, credentials, internal hostnames, private service URLs, or private operational state.

requirements.title: Protocol lane separation
requirements.statement: A public ProtocolLab report MUST keep QUIC transport results separate from HTTP/3 results.

requirements.title: Preserve normalized stack and evidence identity
requirements.statement: A public ProtocolLab report MUST preserve the normalized protocol stack, binding, implementation role, execution variant, and evidence tier associated with each result.

requirements.title: Preserve non-comparable reasons
requirements.statement: A public ProtocolLab report MUST preserve an explicit not-comparable reason when protocol, payload, load, network, role, platform, provenance, topology, validation, telemetry, saturation, or repetition compatibility is absent.
